alertBanner.message: Strait of Hormuz disruption risk elevated — Recession probability: <highlight>{probability}%</highlight>

hormuzDisruption.title: Strait of Hormuz Disruption Elevates Energy Shock Risk
hormuzDisruption.excerpt: Naval tensions in the Persian Gulf have pushed WTI crude above $105, threatening to trigger the Hamilton oil-shock threshold that historically precedes recession within 12 months.

yieldCurveInversion.title: Yield Curve Remains Inverted for 18th Consecutive Month
yieldCurveInversion.excerpt: The 10Y-2Y spread sits at -42bps. The Estrella-Mishkin probit model assigns a 62% probability of recession within the next 12 months based on current spread levels.

sahmRuleWatch.title: Sahm Rule Indicator Approaches Trigger Threshold
sahmRuleWatch.excerpt: The 3-month moving average of unemployment has risen 0.38 percentage points above its 12-month minimum — within 0.12pp of Claudia Sahm's recession trigger at 0.50pp.

dashboard.title: Recession Intelligence Dashboard
dashboard.subtitle: Live composite score — model breakdown, FRED indicators, and alerts in one view.

divergenceWarning.title: Divergence Warning
divergenceWarning.body: Composite score ({composite}%) diverges {diff}pp from Chauvet-Piger ({chauvet}%). Interpret with caution.

analysis.label: Full Analysis
analysis.description: Detailed breakdown of all recession indicators

calculator.label: Recession Calculator
calculator.description: Model custom scenarios with your own inputs

methodology.label: Methodology
methodology.description: How GeoWire builds its composite score

dashboard.footnote: Chauvet-Piger (RECPROUSM156N): {chauvet} · Composite confidence: {confidence} · Data source: {dataSource} · Fetched: {fetchedAt}

oilShock2026.title: Hamilton Oil-Shock Threshold Breached
oilShock2026.summary: WTI crude has exceeded its 36-month maximum. Hamilton NOPI model signals elevated recession risk within 12 months.

yieldUnInversion.title: Yield Curve Un-Inverting — Watch for Bear Steepener
yieldUnInversion.summary: 10Y-3M spread turned positive (+0.60%). Historical pattern: recession often arrives after the curve steepens from inversion.

sahmBelowThreshold.title: Sahm Rule at 0.43pp — Below 0.50 Trigger
sahmBelowThreshold.summary: SAHMCURRENT sits at 0.43pp. The 0.50pp recession trigger has not been crossed, but the trend warrants monitoring.

creditSpreadsStable.title: HY Spreads Stable Below 3.0%
creditSpreadsStable.summary: ICE BofA HY OAS at 2.96%. Credit markets remain complacent relative to equity and rates signals.

phillyFedPositive.title: Philadelphia Fed Leading Index Positive for 3rd Month
phillyFedPositive.summary: USSLIND (Philadelphia Fed Leading Index) remains positive at +0.42%, providing a countervailing signal to yield curve models.

tariffEscalation.title: New Tariff Tranche Expands to Industrial Inputs
tariffEscalation.summary: The latest tariff round covers steel, aluminum, and electronic components — increasing supply-chain inflation risk.

gdpRevision.title: Q4 2025 Real GDP Revised Up to +2.1% Annualized
gdpRevision.summary: The BEA revision reduces recession probability near-term. However, leading indicators continue to soften.

michiganSentiment.title: Michigan Consumer Sentiment Falls to 74.0
michiganSentiment.summary: Consumers increasingly cite inflation and trade uncertainty. Sentiment is a coincident indicator of economic stress.
